user_message: I need the team view on migration architecture. There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 12:37 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 10:57 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 09:04 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-01-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Michelle (Wide World Importers)

[00:01:15] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is migration architecture.

[00:06:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:09:00] Michelle (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:13:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:24:00] Michelle (Wide World Importers): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:32:00] Michelle (Wide World Importers): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:36:00] Michelle (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-I trap (Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-I failure mode (Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-I -- Over-summarizes -- loses specific recent context
